description: Battle of Nisibis (115 AD). Trajan captures the key Mesopotamian fortress during his Parthian campaign.

name: Capture of Ctesiphon (116 AD)
description: Capture of Ctesiphon (116 AD). Trajan captures the Parthian capital, reaching the Persian Gulf.

name: Battle of Seleucia
description: Battle of Seleucia (116 AD). Trajan takes the great Mesopotamian city during his Parthian campaign.

name: Siege of Hatra
description: Siege of Hatra (117 AD). Trajan fails to take the desert fortress, one of his rare setbacks.

name: Siege of Jerusalem (Bar Kokhba)
combatants: Roman Empire vs Jewish Rebels (Bar Kokhba)
description: Fall of Jerusalem (132 AD). Bar Kokhba's rebels seize Jerusalem, forcing a massive Roman response.

name: Battle of Betar
description: Battle of Betar (135 AD). Romans destroy the last stronghold of Bar Kokhba's revolt, ending Jewish statehood.

name: Antonine Advance into Scotland
description: Antonine Wall Campaign (142 AD). Romans advance into Scotland and build the Antonine Wall.

name: Battle of Elegeia
description: Battle of Elegeia (162 AD). Entire Roman legion destroyed by Parthians in Armenia.

name: Battle of Dura Europos
description: Battle of Dura Europos (165 AD). Romans capture the strategic fortress on the Euphrates during Verus's Parthian War.

name: Sack of Ctesiphon (165 AD)
description: Sack of Ctesiphon (165 AD). Romans sack the Parthian capital but bring plague back to the empire.

name: Sack of Seleucia (165 AD)
description: Sack of Seleucia (165 AD). Romans sack the great city during the Parthian War under co-emperor Verus.

name: Battle of Carnuntum
combatants: Roman Empire vs Marcomanni and Quadi
description: Battle of Carnuntum (170 AD). Germanic tribes break through the Danube frontier, devastating the region.

name: Battle on the Frozen Danube
description: Battle on the Frozen Danube (172 AD). Romans fight the Iazyges on the frozen river in a remarkable winter battle.

name: Miracle of the Rain
description: Miracle of the Rain (174 AD). A sudden thunderstorm saves the surrounded Roman army from the Quadi.

name: Battle against the Marcomanni
description: Battle against the Marcomanni (180 AD). Commodus achieves a final victory but ends the wars by making peace.

name: Campaign in Britain
description: Campaign in Britain (184 AD). Ulpius Marcellus repels a major Caledonian incursion south of Hadrian's Wall.

name: Battle of Nicaea
combatants: forces of Septimus Severus vs forces of Pescennius Niger
description: Battle of Nicaea (193 AD) near Nicaea in Anatolia

name: Battle of Cyzicus (193 AD)
description: Battle of Cyzicus (193 AD). Severus's forces defeat Niger's army in Asia Minor.

name: Battle near Cyzicus (193 AD)
description: Battle near Cyzicus (193 AD). First major clash between Severus and Niger for control of the empire.

name: Battle of Issus
description: Battle of Issus (194 AD). Severus defeats Niger to consolidate power in the east.

name: Siege of Byzantium
description: Siege of Byzantium (196 AD). Severus captures and destroys Byzantium after a long siege.

name: Battle of Lugdunum
description: Battle of Lugdunum (197 AD). Largest and bloodiest battle of Severus' civil war.

name: Sack of Ctesiphon (198 AD)
description: Sack of Ctesiphon (198 AD). Severus captures and sacks the Parthian capital during his eastern campaign.

name: Siege of Hatra (199 AD)
description: Siege of Hatra (199 AD). Severus fails to take the desert fortress, as Trajan had before him.

name: Battle of Arbela (216 AD)
description: Battle of Arbela (216 AD). Caracalla's invasion of Parthia yields inconclusive results before his assassination.

name: Battle of Nisibis
description: Battle of Nisibis (217 AD). Parthian victory forces Rome to pay for peace.

name: Battle of Antioch
combatants: Roman Empire Macrinus vs Roman Empire Elagabalus
description: Battle of Antioch (218 AD) near near Antioch, Syria

name: Battle of Immae (218 AD)
description: Battle of Immae (218 AD). The teenage pretender Elagabalus defeats Emperor Macrinus near Antioch.

name: Battle at Hormuz
combatants: Parthian Empire (Roman ally) vs Sassanid Persia
description: Battle of Hormuz (224 AD). Ardashir I defeats Artabanus IV, replacing the Parthian Empire with the Sassanid dynasty.

name: Roman Campaign to Ctesiphon (233 AD)
description: Campaign to Ctesiphon (233 AD). Alexander Severus's inconclusive Mesopotamian campaign against Ardashir I.

name: Battle of Carthage (238 AD)
combatants: Gordian I vs Legio III Augusta
description: Battle of Carthage (238 AD). Gordian I's rebellion is crushed; he commits suicide after his son's death.

name: Siege of Aquileia (238 AD)
combatants: Maximinus Thrax vs City of Aquileia
description: Siege of Aquileia (238 AD). The city resists; Maximinus's own soldiers murder him.

name: Battle of Resaena
description: Battle of Resaena (243 AD) near Resaena, Mesopotamia (Ras al-Ayn, Syria)

name: Battle of Resaena (243 AD)
description: Battle of Resaena (243 AD). Roman victory against the Sassanids under the able prefect Timesitheus.

name: Battle of Misiche
description: Battle of Misiche (244 AD). Emperor Gordian III dies (possibly murdered) after defeat by Shapur I.

name: Battle of Verona (249 AD)
description: Battle of Verona (249 AD). Decius defeats and kills Philip the Arab to seize the throne.

name: Battle of Philippopolis
description: Battle of Philippopolis (250 AD)

name: Siege of Philippopolis (250 AD)
description: Siege of Philippopolis (250 AD). Goths sack the city; Priscus declares himself emperor under Gothic patronage.

name: Battle of Abrittus
description: Battle of Abrittus (251 AD) near Moesia Inferior (modern Razgrad, Bulgaria)

name: Battle of Abritus
description: Battle of Abritus (251 AD). Emperor Decius killed, first Roman emperor to die in battle against barbarians.

name: Battle of Forum Terebronii
description: Battle of Abrittus/Forum Terebronii (251 AD). Emperor Decius killed in battle, the first emperor to fall to barbarians.

name: Battle of Barbalissos
description: Battle of Barbalissos (253 AD). Shapur I destroys a Roman army of 60,000 on the Euphrates.

name: Battle of Emesa (253 AD)
combatants: Roman Empire (Uranius Antoninus) vs Sassanid Empire
description: Battle of Emesa (253 AD). Failed Roman attempt to halt Shapur I's invasion of Syria.

name: Battle of Spoleto
description: Battle of Spoleto (253 AD). Aemilianus is murdered by his own troops as Valerian approaches.

name: Defense of Emesa
description: Defense of Emesa (254 AD). Local Roman usurper successfully defends the city against Sassanid invasion.

name: Battle of Mediolanum
description: Battle of Mediolanum (259 AD) near Mediolanum (modern Milan), Italy

name: Battle of Edessa
description: Battle of Edessa (260 AD). Emperor Valerian captured alive by Shapur I - unique humiliation.

name: Rise of Postumus
description: Battle of Cologne (260 AD). Postumus defeats and kills Saloninus, establishing the breakaway Gallic Empire.

name: Battle of Milan (260 AD)
description: Battle near Milan (260 AD). Gallienus defeats the usurper Ingenuus on the Danube frontier.

name: Battle of Mursa (260 AD)
description: Battle of Mursa (260 AD). Gallienus defeats another Danubian usurper, Regalianus.

name: Battle of Verona (267 AD)
description: Battle of Verona (267 AD). Gallienus defeats an Alamannic invasion force near Verona.

name: Battle of Naissus
description: Battle of Naissus (268 AD). Decisive Roman victory over a massive Gothic invasion force.

name: Battle of Lake Benacus
description: Battle of Lake Benacus (268 AD) near Lake Garda, modern Italy

name: Battle of Nessos
description: Battle of Nessos (268 AD). Gallienus routs a Gothic force in Thrace before his assassination.

name: Revolt of Laelianus
description: Revolt of Laelianus (269 AD). Postumus defeats the usurper but is killed by his own troops for forbidding a sack.

name: Battle of Placentia
description: Battle of Placentia (271 AD) near near modern Piacenza, Italy

name: Battle of Fano
description: Battle of Fano (271 AD) near on the Metaurus River near Fano, Italy

name: Battle of Pavia (271)
description: Battle of Pavia (271) (271 AD) near near Pavia, Lombardy, Italy

name: Battle near Daphne
description: Battle near Daphne (271 AD). Palmyrene forces under Zenobia's general invade and conquer Roman Egypt.

name: Battle of Emesa
description: Battle of Emesa (272 AD). Aurelian defeats Queen Zenobia, reuniting the empire.

name: Battle of Immae
description: Battle of Immae (272 AD). Aurelian defeats Zenobia's cavalry in Syria, beginning Palmyra's fall.

name: Siege of Palmyra
description: Siege of Palmyra (272 AD). Aurelian captures the desert city, ending Zenobia's breakaway empire.

name: Battle of Chalons (274 AD)
description: Battle of Chalons (274 AD). Aurelian defeats Tetricus, reuniting the Gallic Empire with Rome.

name: Battle of Caenophrurium
description: Battle of Caenophrurium (274 AD). Aurelian wins his final major battle against Gothic raiders in Thrace.

name: Battle against Alamanni in Gaul
description: Battle in Gaul (277 AD). Probus defeats the Alamanni and drives them back across the Rhine.

name: Battle against Vandals on the Danube
description: Battle on the Danube (278 AD). Probus defeats the Vandals and resettles them within the empire.

name: Revolt of Saturninus
description: Revolt of Saturninus (280 AD). Probus suppresses the rebellion of Saturninus at Cologne.

name: Capture of Ctesiphon (283 AD)
description: Capture of Ctesiphon (283 AD). Carus easily takes the Sassanid capital before his mysterious death.

name: Battle of Margum
description: Battle of Margum (285 AD). Diocletian defeats Carinus (who is killed by his own officers) to become emperor.

name: Battle of the Margus (285 AD)
description: Battle of the Margus (285 AD). Diocletian wins after Carinus is murdered by his own tribune.

name: Campaigns against Carausius
description: Campaigns against Carausius (293 AD). Constantius besieges Boulogne, reclaiming it from the British usurper.

name: Reconquest of Britain
description: Reconquest of Britain (296 AD). Constantius invades and defeats Allectus, restoring Britain to the empire.

name: Siege of Alexandria
description: Siege of Alexandria (298 AD). Diocletian personally captures Alexandria after an eight-month siege.

name: Battle of Callinicum (298 AD)
description: Battle of Callinicum (298 AD). Galerius defeats the Sassanids decisively after an earlier setback.

name: Battle of Satala
description: Battle of Satala (298 AD). Galerius captures the Sassanid king's harem and treasury in a surprise attack.

name: Battle of Lingones
description: Battle of Lingones (298 AD). Constantius defeats the Alamanni after a close-fought battle in Gaul.

name: Battle of Vindonissa
description: Battle of Vindonissa (298 AD). Constantius drives the Alamanni back across the Rhine at modern Windisch.

name: Battle near Eboracum
description: Campaign near Eboracum (306 AD). Constantius campaigns against the Picts before dying at York; Constantine acclaimed emperor.

name: Battle of Milvian Bridge
description: Battle of Milvian Bridge (312 AD) near Ponte Milvio, Rome

name: Battle of the Milvian Bridge
description: Battle of the Milvian Bridge (312 AD). Constantine's vision and victory, leading to Christianity's rise.

name: Battle of Turin
description: Battle of Turin (312 AD). Constantine defeats Maxentius's cavalry force on his march to Rome.

name: Battle of Verona (312 AD)
description: Battle of Verona (312 AD). Constantine defeats the strong garrison before proceeding to Rome.

name: Battle of Tzirallum
combatants: forces of Licinius vs forces of Maximinus
description: Battle of Tzirallum (313 AD) near near Heraclea Sintica

name: Battle of Campus Ergenus
description: Battle of Campus Ergenus (313 AD). Licinius defeats Maximinus Daia, consolidating the eastern empire.

name: Battle of Cibalae
description: Battle of Cibalae (314 AD). Constantine defeats Licinius in the first of their wars.

name: Battle of Mardia
description: Battle of Mardia (316 AD) near the basin of Ardas River, Greece or Harmanli, Bulgaria

name: Battle of Campus Ardiensis
description: Battle of Campus Ardiensis (316 AD). Inconclusive engagement leading to a temporary peace between Constantine and Licinius.

name: Battle of the Hellespont
description: Battle of the Hellespont (324 AD) near the Hellespont (Dardanelles)

name: Battle of Chrysopolis
description: Battle of Chrysopolis (324 AD). Constantine defeats Licinius, becoming sole emperor.

name: Battle of Adrianople (324 AD)
description: Battle of Adrianople (324 AD). Constantine defeats Licinius in a major land battle before the siege of Byzantium.

name: Battle of Hileia
description: Battle of Hileia (343 AD). Constantius campaigns against Sarmatian raiders on the Danube.

name: Battle of Singara
description: Battle of Singara (344 AD) near Singara, Mesopotamia

name: Battle of Mursa Major
description: Battle of Mursa Major (351 AD). One of the bloodiest Roman civil war battles.

name: Battle of Cibalae (351 AD)
description: Confrontation at Cibalae (351 AD). Vetranio's troops desert to Constantius without a battle.

name: Battle of Mons Seleucus
description: Battle of Mons Seleucus (353 AD) near La Bâtie-Montsaléon, France

name: Battle of Reims
description: Battle of Reims (356 AD) near Reims, modern France

name: Battle of Brumath
description: Battle of Brumath (356 AD). Julian's first victory, driving the Alamanni from Alsace.

name: Siege of Sens
description: Siege of Sens (356 AD). Julian successfully defends the city against an Alamannic siege.

name: Battle of Strasbourg
description: Battle of Strasbourg (357 AD). Julian crushes a large Alamanni confederation on the Rhine.

name: Battle of Amida
description: Battle of Amida (359 AD). Shapur II captures the fortress after a brutal 73-day siege.

name: Siege of Aquileia
description: Siege of Aquileia (361 AD). Julian's forces besiege the city during his march east against Constantius.

name: Battle of Ctesiphon
description: Battle of Ctesiphon (363 AD). Julian wins before the Sassanid capital but retreats; dies shortly after.

name: Battle of Samarra
description: Battle of Samarra (363 AD). Julian is mortally wounded during the retreat from Persia, ending his campaign.

name: Battle of Maiozamalcha
description: Battle of Maiozamalcha (363 AD). Julian storms a fortified Sassanid city on the Tigris en route to Ctesiphon.

name: Battle of Thyatira
description: Battle of Thyatira (366 AD) near Thyatira, Phrygia (modern Turkey)

name: Battle of Chalons (366 AD)
description: Battle of Chalons (366 AD). Romans defeat an Alamannic raiding party in Gaul.

name: Battle of Solicinium
description: Battle of Solicinium (367 AD) near Southwest-Germany, south of the Limes, see article

name: Battle of the Willows
description: Battle of the Willows (377 AD) near Ad Salices (exact unknown), in Moesia, Bulgaria

name: Battle of Dibaltum
description: Battle of Dibaltum (377 AD). Gothic raiders defeat a Roman force in Thrace before Adrianople.

name: Battle of Adrianople
description: Battle of Adrianople (378 AD). Catastrophic Roman defeat; Emperor Valens killed. Beginning of the end.

name: Battle of Argentovaria
description: Battle of Argentovaria (378 AD) near Argentovaria, near Colmar, modern France

name: Battle of Thessalonica
description: Battle of Thessalonica (380 AD)

name: Gothic Raids after Adrianople
description: Gothic raids (380 AD). Goths ravage Thessaly and Macedonia after Adrianople before Theodosius makes peace.

name: Battle of the Save
combatants: Eastern Roman Empire vs forces of Magnus Maximus
description: Battle of the Save (388 AD) near Sava river, near Siscia, modern Croatia

name: Battle of Poetovio
description: Battle of Poetovio (388 AD). Theodosius defeats and captures the usurper Magnus Maximus.

name: Battle of the Frigidus
description: Battle of the Frigidus (394 AD). Theodosius reunites the empire for the last time.

name: Battle of Eleusis
description: Battle of Eleusis (396 AD). Stilicho confronts Alaric in Greece but allows him to escape.

name: Battle of Pollentia
description: Battle of Pollentia (402 AD). Stilicho checks Alaric's invasion of Italy.

name: Battle of Verona
description: Battle of Verona (402 AD) near near Verona, Italy

name: Battle of Mainz
description: Battle of Mainz (406 AD) near Mainz, modern Germany

name: Battle of Faesulae (406 AD)
description: Battle of Faesulae (406 AD). Stilicho destroys Radagaisus's massive Gothic invasion of Italy.

name: Sack of Rome by Alaric
description: Sack of Rome (410 AD). Alaric's Visigoths sack Rome for the first time in 800 years.

name: Battle of Vienne
description: Battle of Vienne (411 AD). Barbarian-backed usurper Jovinus seizes power in Gaul.

name: Battle of Arles
description: Battle of Arles (425 AD). Eastern forces defeat the usurper Joannes, placing Valentinian III on the throne.

name: Siege of Hippo Regius
description: Siege of Hippo Regius (430 AD). Vandals besiege Augustine's city; he dies during the siege.

name: Battle of Ravenna
combatants: forces of Bonifacius vs forces of Flavius Aëtius
description: Battle of Ravenna (432 AD) near near Rimini, present-day Italy

name: Battle of Rimini
description: Battle of Rimini (432 AD). Aetius loses to Bonifacius but Bonifacius dies of wounds, leaving Aetius supreme.

name: Battle of Narbonne
description: Battle of Narbonne (436 AD) near Narbonne, France

name: Battle of Mons Colubrarius
description: Battle of Mons Colubrarius (436 AD). Roman-Hunnic forces relieve the siege of Narbonne.

name: First Battle of Toulouse
description: First Battle of Toulouse (439 AD). Roman general Litorius defeated and captured attacking the Visigothic capital.

name: Battle of the Utus
description: Battle of the Utus (447 AD) near near Vit river, Bulgaria

name: Battle of the Catalaunian Plains
description: Battle of the Catalaunian Plains (451 AD). Aetius and Visigoths halt Attila the Hun in Gaul.

name: Battle of Orleans
description: Battle of Orleans (451 AD). Aetius and the Visigoths relieve Orleans from Attila's siege.

name: Battle of the Catalaunian Fields (detail)
description: Battle of the Catalaunian Fields (451 AD). Last great Roman victory; Aetius and allies halt Attila in Gaul.

name: Battle of Nedao
description: Battle of Nedao (454 AD). Germanic tribes, allied with Rome, destroy Hunnic power after Attila's death.

name: Sack of Rome
description: Sack of Rome (455 AD) near Rome

name: Sack of Rome by Vandals
description: Sack of Rome (455 AD). Genseric's Vandals plunder Rome for two weeks.

name: Hunnic Collapse at Nedao
description: Battle of Nedao (455 AD). Germanic tribes destroy Hunnic power, freeing central Europe from Hunnic domination.

name: Battle of Placentia (456 AD)
combatants: Western Roman Empire and Visigoths vs Suevi
description: Battle of Placentia (456 AD). Ricimer defeats the emperor Avitus, becoming the power behind the throne.

name: Battle of Cartagena
description: Battle of Cartagena (461 AD). Vandals destroy Majorian's invasion fleet, dooming his reconquest attempt.

name: Battle of Cap Bon
description: Battle of Cap Bon (468 AD). Vandals use fire ships to destroy the massive Roman-Byzantine fleet.

name: Battle of Ravenna (476 AD)
description: Battle of Ravenna (476 AD). Odoacer deposes the last Western Roman emperor, ending the Western Empire.

name: Battle of Yarmouk
description: A six-day disaster on the Yarmouk river that lost Syria and the Levant to the Arabs forever.

name: Arab Siege of Constantinople
description: The first great Arab siege, broken by the walls and the terrifying new weapon of Greek fire.

name: Second Arab Siege of Constantinople
description: Leo III shattered the largest Muslim assault on the city — a turning point for all of Europe.

name: Battle of Akroinon
description: A decisive victory in Anatolia that ended the tide of Umayyad invasions.

name: Battle of Lalakaon
combatants: Byzantine Empire vs Emirate of Melitene
description: Annihilated the raiding emir Umar al-Aqta and swung the eastern frontier in Byzantium's favour.

name: Battle of Kleidion
description: Basil II's crushing victory that earned him the name "Bulgar-Slayer" and ended the Bulgarian war.

name: Battle of Manzikert
description: The catastrophe that captured an emperor and opened the Anatolian heartland to the Turks.

name: Battle of Dyrrhachium
description: Robert Guiscard's Normans routed Alexios I, though the empire would recover under him.

name: Battle of Myriokephalon
combatants: Byzantine Empire vs Sultanate of Rum
description: An ambush in a mountain pass that ended Byzantine hopes of retaking Anatolia.

name: Sack of Constantinople
description: Christian crusaders stormed and looted the capital — a wound the empire never truly recovered from.

name: Recapture of Constantinople
description: A small Byzantine force slipped into the city and restored the empire under Michael VIII.

name: Battle of Pelekanon
description: The last field attempt to save Byzantine Bithynia failed against Orhan's Ottomans.

name: Fall of Constantinople
description: Mehmed II's cannon breached the Theodosian Walls; the last Roman emperor fell and the empire ended.
